In this section, we describe the proposed multimodal emoi=0
tion recognition framework for the EXPR task. Our model
consists of five components: (1) unimodal feature extrac- where d is the dilation factor and k is the kernel size. This
tion using pre-trained backbones, (2) temporal visual mod- formulation enlarges the temporal receptive field without

cation, and (5) text-guided contrastive learning. Overview of the proposed multimodal emotion recognition framework.
title: Multimodal Emotion Recognition via Bi-directional Cross-Attention and Temporal Modeling

text:
The model encodes video frames and audio signals using
frozen CLIP and Wav2Vec 2.0 backbones, respectively. The visual features are further refined by a temporal convolutional network, while
the audio features are projected through an adapter layer. The resulting multimodal representations are integrated via bi-directional crossattention, followed by temporal pooling and an MLP classifier for expression recognition. During training, text prompts are additionally
used to impose a text-guided contrastive objective.
